repo.name: scrapy/scrapy
repo.scope: the Scrapy web-crawling framework (Python, Twisted async)

reference_claims.text: There is a Scrapy execution Engine component.
There is a Scheduler component.
There is a Downloader component.
There are Spiders: custom classes (a Spider base class) written by users.
There is an Item Pipeline component.
There are Downloader middlewares (a downloader-middleware layer/manager).
There are Spider middlewares (a spider-middleware layer/manager).
The Engine is responsible for controlling the data flow between all components of the system and triggering events when certain actions occur.
The Scheduler receives requests from the engine and enqueues them for feeding them later (back to the engine) when the engine requests them.
The Downloader is responsible for fetching web pages and feeding them to the engine (which feeds them to the spiders).
Spiders parse responses and extract items from them or additional requests to follow.
The Item Pipeline processes items once they have been extracted by the spiders (typical tasks: cleansing, validation, persistence).
Downloader middlewares are hooks that sit between the Engine and the Downloader and process requests (Engine->Downloader) and responses (Downloader->Engine).
Spider middlewares are hooks that sit between the Engine and the Spiders and process spider input (responses) and output (items and requests).
The Engine schedules requests in the Scheduler and asks it for the next requests to crawl (Engine<->Scheduler).
The Engine sends requests to the Downloader and receives the generated Responses (Engine<->Downloader).
The Engine sends Responses to the Spider for processing and receives scraped items and new requests (Engine<->Spider).
The Engine sends processed (scraped) items to the Item Pipelines.
All requests and responses passing between the Engine and the Downloader pass through the Downloader middlewares.
All spider input (responses) and output (items/requests) passing between the Engine and the Spiders pass through the Spider middlewares.
The data flow is controlled centrally by the execution engine; the engine mediates between the other components rather than them being wired directly to each other.
The crawl loop repeats: the engine keeps asking the scheduler for the next requests until there are no more requests from the scheduler.
The crawl begins with the Engine getting the initial Requests to crawl from the Spider (start requests).
Scrapy is written with Twisted, an event-driven networking framework, and is implemented using non-blocking (asynchronous) code for concurrency.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Architecture; architecture.md > Overview
emitted_claims.text: Scrapy is a single hatchling-built Python package (name = "Scrapy"; build-backend hatchling).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Architecture; critical-facts.md > CLI
emitted_claims.text: It exposes exactly one console entry point: scrapy = "scrapy.cmdline:execute".

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Architecture; architecture.md > Overview
emitted_claims.text: The hub is the ExecutionEngine, which controls the Scheduler, Downloader and Spider; its constructor instantiates and wires a Scheduler, Downloader and Scraper.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Architecture; architecture.md
emitted_claims.text: Request/Response objects flow through two pluggable middleware chains: DownloaderMiddlewareManager on the network side and SpiderMiddlewareManager around the user's Spider.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Architecture; architecture.md
emitted_claims.text: All middleware chains share one MiddlewareManager ABC, subclassed inward by DownloaderMiddlewareManager, SpiderMiddlewareManager, ExtensionManager and ItemPipelineManager.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Architecture; crawler component doc
emitted_claims.text: Lifecycle/ownership is held by the Crawler / CrawlerProcess family; a Crawler wires one spider to settings/engine while Runner/Process own reactor + event-loop startup.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Architecture; settings component doc
emitted_claims.text: Configuration is a priority-aware Settings system (BaseSettings/Settings) whose default_settings.py registers the swappable backends as import-string literals.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core (engine / scheduler / scraper / spider-middleware runner) is the central orchestration sub-package.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core.downloader (downloader + handlers + TLS) owns the network-fetch concern: Downloader.fetch runs the middleware chain, throttles per slot, dispatches to per-scheme handlers.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; http2 component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core.http2 is a self-contained Twisted HTTP/2 client stack that pools connections and multiplexes requests onto streams via hyper-h2.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.crawler (Crawler + Runner/Process lifecycle) is the top-level crawl lifecycle module.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; cmdline component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.cmdline + scrapy.commands (CLI) parse argv, resolve a ScrapyCommand subclass, optionally build a crawler process, and dispatch run().

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.spiders is the user-facing extension point: Spider base plus CrawlSpider/XMLFeedSpider/CSVFeedSpider/SitemapSpider.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; downloadermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.downloadermiddlewares holds the pluggable downloader middlewares (cookies, redirect, retry, robotstxt, httpcompression, httpproxy, httpcache, offsite, ...).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; spidermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.spidermiddlewares holds the built-in spider middlewares (start, httperror, referer, urllength, depth) between scraper and spider.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.pipelines does post-scrape item processing: ItemPipelineManager + MediaPipeline ABC with FilesPipeline/ImagesPipeline.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.extensions holds optional signal-driven add-ons (closespider, corestats, throttle, memusage, telnet) plus FeedExporter and HTTP-cache.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; http component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.http is the HTTP data model: Request/Response + typed subclasses + Headers/CookieJar.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; settings component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.settings is the priority-aware freezable config: BaseSettings (MutableMapping) + Settings + default registry.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.utils is the leaf shared infra: reactor install, Deferred<->coroutine bridge, load_object, conf/log/parsing.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; selector/linkextractors component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.selector + scrapy.linkextractors do content extraction: Selector/SelectorList over parsel + LxmlLinkExtractor (LinkExtractor).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; item/loader/exporters component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy item + loader + exporters is the structured-data layer: Item/Field model + ItemLoader + JSON/XML/CSV/Pickle exporters.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; queues/dupefilter/spiderloader component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy queues + dupefilter + spiderloader are the pluggable scheduler backends: priority queues, disk/memory queues, RFPDupeFilter, SpiderLoader.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; contracts component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.contracts is a docstring-driven spider-callback test framework run as unittest cases.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Component registry; signals/middleware/addons component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy signals + middleware base + addons is shared plug-in machinery: MiddlewareManager ABC, PyDispatcher SignalManager, AddonManager.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; technical-dependencies.md
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core depends on scrapy.core.downloader (load_object DOWNLOADER, .fetch/.needs_backout/.close).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; technical-dependencies.md
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core depends on scrapy.pipelines (load_object ITEM_PROCESSOR, process_item_async).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; technical-dependencies.md
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core depends on scrapy.http (import + isinstance type-dispatch on Request/Response).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; technical-dependencies.md
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core depends on scrapy queues + dupefilter + spiderloader (ScrapyPriorityQueue, BaseDupeFilter via the scheduler).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; http2 component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core.downloader depends on scrapy.core.http2 (the H2 handler imports H2Agent/H2ConnectionPool).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; http2 component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core.http2 depends on scrapy.core.downloader for the TLS context-factory helper (_AcceptableProtocolsContextFactory).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; technical-dependencies.md
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.crawler depends on scrapy.core (imports + constructs ExecutionEngine).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; cmdline component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.cmdline + scrapy.commands depend on scrapy.crawler (import + runtime-instantiation of CrawlerProcess/AsyncCrawlerProcess).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; cmdline/contracts component docs
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.cmdline + scrapy.commands depend on scrapy.contracts (ContractsManager via the check command).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; downloadermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.downloadermiddlewares are run by scrapy.core.downloader (DownloaderMiddlewareManager runs the hooks).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; downloadermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.downloadermiddlewares depends on scrapy.core (RobotsTxtMiddleware awaits engine.download_async for robots.txt).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; downloadermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.downloadermiddlewares (redirect.py) depends on scrapy.spidermiddlewares (borrows RefererMiddleware for redirect Referer) ג€” the one downloader->spider-middleware coupling.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; spidermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.spidermiddlewares is consumed by scrapy.core via inversion: SpiderMiddlewareManager loads these by import-path; there is no compile-time edge out.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.pipelines depends on scrapy signals + middleware base + addons (ItemPipelineManager subclasses MiddlewareManager).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.pipelines depends on scrapy.core (MediaPipeline awaits engine.download_async for media).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.extensions depends on scrapy.crawler as the sole injected dependency (TYPE_CHECKING + from_crawler).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.extensions depends on scrapy.core.downloader (AutoThrottle reads and mutates downloader slots).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.spiders depends on scrapy.http (imports Request/Response).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.spiders (CrawlSpider) depends on scrapy.selector + scrapy.linkextractors (LinkExtractor/Selector).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.utils reaches into scrapy.core internals via get_engine_status (ExecutionEngine debug helper).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; settings component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.settings references other components only as default-registry import-string literals, NOT compile-time imports.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; queues component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy queues + dupefilter + spiderloader reach into scrapy.core.downloader via crawler.engine.downloader slots (DownloaderAwarePriorityQueue).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Technical dependencies; http component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.http depends on scrapy.selector via a lazy import to break a cycle (TextResponse.selector).

emitted_claims.source_region: technical-dependencies.md > scrapy.settings
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.settings has only two compile-time scrapy imports: scrapy.utils and scrapy.exceptions.

emitted_claims.source_region: technical-dependencies.md > scrapy.utils
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.core.engine imports 7 distinct scrapy.utils modules, corroborating utils is a depended-upon leaf.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices; technical-dependencies.md > External deps
emitted_claims.text: Twisted is the core async/reactor substrate; declared dependency Twisted>=21.7.0.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Critical to know; critical-facts.md
emitted_claims.text: The default Twisted reactor is twisted.internet.asyncioreactor.AsyncioSelectorReactor; the engine can switch to the asyncio loop at runtime.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices
emitted_claims.text: HTTP transport: built-in HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 download handlers (http11.HTTP11DownloadHandler, http2.HTTP2DownloadHandler).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices
emitted_claims.text: Additional transports/schemes: data:, file:, s3:, ftp: download handlers are registered in DOWNLOAD_HANDLERS_BASE.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices; selector component doc
emitted_claims.text: HTML/XML parsing & extraction: lxml + parsel + cssselect; Selector wraps parsel's _ParselSelector.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices
emitted_claims.text: TLS/crypto: pyOpenSSL + cryptography + service_identity.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices; signals component doc
emitted_claims.text: Signals/event bus: PyDispatcher (CPython) / PyPyDispatcher (PyPy) behind SignalManager.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices
emitted_claims.text: Request queues / scheduling: queuelib disk+memory queues + priority queues; default priority queue is DownloaderAwarePriorityQueue and default dupefilter is RFPDupeFilter.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices
emitted_claims.text: robots.txt parsing uses the protego parser (ProtegoRobotParser).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices; item component doc
emitted_claims.text: Item adaptation/loading: itemadapter + itemloaders + w3lib; ItemLoader is a thin subclass of upstream itemloaders.ItemLoader.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices; http component doc
emitted_claims.text: Secure XML parsing uses defusedxml; XmlRpcRequest hardens parsing with defusedxml.monkey_patch.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices
emitted_claims.text: Interface contracts use zope.interface (e.g. IFeedStorage, ISpiderLoader verifyClass-checked).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices; License
emitted_claims.text: License & runtime floor: BSD-3-Clause, requires-python >=3.10.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Technology choices; http2 component doc
emitted_claims.text: hyper-h2 (h2) is a hard import in the HTTP/2 stack but is NOT in [project].dependencies ג€” HTTP/2 is effectively optional/transitive.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Summary; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Scrapy ships no fixed/bundled database; persistence is pluggable file/ftp/s3 feed-export and httpcache backends.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Scheduler & queues
emitted_claims.text: Scheduler job-directory persistence: a requests.queue/ dir + active.json (priority-queue startprios state), read on open and written on close, only when JOBDIR is set.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Scheduler & queues; queues component doc
emitted_claims.text: On-disk request queues use queuelib FifoDiskQueue/LifoDiskQueue subclasses that auto-mkdir the parent and store serialized request dicts.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Scheduler & queues
emitted_claims.text: Disk-queue serialization uses pickle protocol 4 or marshal; PicklingError/AttributeError/TypeError are normalized to ValueError.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Dupefilter
emitted_claims.text: Dupefilter JOBDIR persistence: a requests.seen file (one hex fingerprint per line), append+read, line-buffered/write_through, pre-loaded into a set on init.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Dupefilter; queues component doc
emitted_claims.text: De-dup identity = request fingerprint (canonical url+method+body, hex) via the pluggable RequestFingerprinterProtocol (default RequestFingerprinter).

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Media/files/images stores; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: FilesStoreProtocol has four URI-scheme-selected backends in STORE_SCHEMES: FSFilesStore (local), S3FilesStore (s3), GCSFilesStore (gs), FTPFilesStore (ftp).

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Media/files/images stores; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: Content-addressed media paths: <basedir>/full/<sha1(url)><ext> for files and full/<sha1(url)>.jpg + thumbs/<thumb_id>/<sha1(url)>.jpg for images.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > HTTP cache backends; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: HTTP cache filesystem backend writes meta/pickled_meta/response_headers/body/request_headers/body under <cachedir>/<spider.name>/<key[0:2]>/<key>, optional gzip.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > HTTP cache backends; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: HTTP cache DBM backend is a per-spider DBM db <cachedir>/<spider.name>.db storing pickled {key}_data + {key}_time.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Spider job state; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: Spider job state pickles spider.state to <jobdir>/spider.state on close and unpickles it on open.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Feed exports; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: Feed exports use pluggable storage backends (local file / stdout / S3 / GCS / FTP); blocking backends stage to a NamedTemporaryFile then upload off-thread.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Pickle trust boundary; critical-facts.md > Security
emitted_claims.text: Persistence uses Python pickle protocol 4 in three places (spider state, DBM cache, filesystem cache meta), with loads marked unsafe (# noqa: S301) ג€” a trust boundary on cache/jobdir contents.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Config files / environment; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.utils reads (does not own) the on-disk scrapy.cfg via ConfigParser, searching project dir + /etc/scrapy.cfg + c:\scrapy\scrapy.cfg + $XDG_CONFIG_HOME + ~/.scrapy.cfg.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > Cross-hook scratch store; downloadermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: Request.meta is used as a cross-hook/cross-retry scratch store (redirect_times/ttl, retry_times, cached_response, proxy/_auth_proxy).

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md > In-memory state; downloader component doc
emitted_claims.text: Persistent Twisted HTTP connection pool, in-process, maxPersistentPerHost = CONCURRENT_REQUESTS_PER_DOMAIN; HTTP/2 uses H2ConnectionPool.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Scheduler interface is enforced structurally not by inheritance: BaseSchedulerMeta duck-types issubclass/isinstance (has_pending_requests/enqueue_request/next_request) and the engine raises TypeError if SCHEDULER is not a BaseScheduler.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Collaborators are pluggable via settings + load_object, never hard-wired (DOWNLOADER, SCHEDULER, ITEM_PROCESSOR, DUPEFILTER_CLASS, SCHEDULER_*_QUEUE); build_from_crawler prefers from_crawler else __init__.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; core component doc
emitted_claims.text: The request-processing loop is a debounced CallLaterOnce(self._start_scheduled_requests) (nextcall) plus a 5s heartbeat LoopingCall that re-schedules it.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; core component doc
emitted_claims.text: Backpressure boundary: the engine stops sending when needs_backout() is true, ORing downloader and scraper-slot backout signals.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; core component doc
emitted_claims.text: Default request order is LIFO/DFO: the default Scheduler stores requests in priority queues that with default settings behave as a LIFO stack giving depth-first crawl order.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; queues component doc
emitted_claims.text: Priority convention: stored priority is -request.priority and LOWER numbers are higher priority, one internal queue per priority value.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; core component doc
emitted_claims.text: Spider idleness + graceful close: spider_is_idle() checks scraper-slot idle + downloader inactive + start-iterator exhausted + scheduler empty; _spider_idle honors a DontCloseSpider veto and reads a custom reason from CloseSpider.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Engine/scheduling; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Async-API migration boundary: every public sync method is deprecated and delegates to an _async coroutine variant.

emitted_claims.source_region: data-layer.md; core component doc
emitted_claims.text: Disk-queue is preferred over memory on enqueue: serializable requests go to disk, non-serializable fall back to memory and bump scheduler/unserializable.

emitted_claims.source_region: core component doc > Close ordering; architecture.md > Data-flow spine
emitted_claims.text: Graceful close is best-effort and ordered: slot -> downloader -> scraper -> scheduler -> fires spider_closed -> closes stats -> runs spider_closed_callback, each wrapped in its own try/except.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Crawler/lifecycle; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: One reactor per process: only the first crawler installs the reactor (self._initialized_reactor); install must precede any twisted.internet.reactor import.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Crawler/lifecycle; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Reactor-enabled vs reactorless are mutually exclusive, hard-enforced with RuntimeError; CrawlerRunner refuses TWISTED_REACTOR_ENABLED=False.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Crawler/lifecycle; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Module-level import of twisted.internet.reactor is lint-banned (because importing it installs the global reactor); the reactor is imported lazily inside functions; ReactorImportHook forbids it in reactorless mode.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Crawler/lifecycle; crawler component doc
emitted_claims.text: crawl()/crawl_async() are once-only per Crawler instance, guarded by self.crawling/self._started raising RuntimeError.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Crawler/lifecycle; crawler component doc
emitted_claims.text: Settings are frozen after _apply_settings (idempotent-guarded by if self.settings.frozen: return).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Crawler/lifecycle; crawler component doc
emitted_claims.text: Two-phase signal shutdown: first signal -> graceful stop; second signal -> forced unclean kill; mirrored on the reactorless path.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Crawler/lifecycle; crawler component doc
emitted_claims.text: _apply_reactorless_default_settings swaps HTTP/HTTPS download handlers to HttpxDownloadHandler and disables telnet+FTP when running without a Twisted reactor.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md > Critical to know; crawler component doc
emitted_claims.text: Dual API surface by design: Deferred-based CrawlerRunner/CrawlerProcess parallel coroutine-based AsyncCrawlerRunner/AsyncCrawlerProcess.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Settings; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: The settings priority ladder is the layering invariant: SETTINGS_PRIORITIES = {default:0, command:10, addon:15, project:20, spider:30, cmdline:40}; a set only takes effect if incoming priority >= stored priority.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Settings; settings component doc
emitted_claims.text: Immutability gate: every mutator routes through _assert_mutability, which raises TypeError once freeze() sets self.frozen=True.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Settings; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: The _BASE settings convention drives chain order: framework component lists live in *_BASE settings, user overrides in the un-suffixed twin; integer priorities (not import order) order the chains; getwithbase composes name_BASE then name so user entries win.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Settings; settings component doc
emitted_claims.text: Settings is a low-level leaf: it imports only scrapy.exceptions and scrapy.utils, and must NOT import the components it configures (referenced only as import-string defaults to avoid cycles).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Settings; settings component doc
emitted_claims.text: Typed accessors coerce env-var-style strings: getbool accepts 0/1/'0'/'1'/True/False/'true'/'false'; getlist splits on ','; getdict/getdictorlist parse JSON.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains; downloader component doc
emitted_claims.text: Downloader hook contract: process_request/process_response/process_exception must return None/Response/Request else _InvalidOutput is raised; None=continue, Response/Request=short-circuit.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains; downloader component doc
emitted_claims.text: Symmetric chain ordering: process_request appended, process_response/process_exception appendleft (reverse order).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Priority-ordered downloader chain (not import order): DOWNLOADER_MIDDLEWARES_BASE Offsite=50, RobotsTxt=100, Retry=550, Redirect=600, Cookies=700, HttpProxy=750, HttpCache=900.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains; spidermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: Spider-side priority order in SPIDER_MIDDLEWARES_BASE: start=25, httperror=50, referer=700, urllength=800, depth=900.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains
emitted_claims.text: from_crawler is the only construction boundary; middlewares raise NotConfigured to opt out when their enable-setting is off.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains; AGENTS.md > Critical to know
emitted_claims.text: Spider middlewares never import the core runner ג€” inversion of control: SpiderMiddlewareManager loads them by import-path and dispatches their hooks.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains; core component doc
emitted_claims.text: Spider middleware output-protocol enforcement: middleware lacking async spider-output support raises TypeError; process_spider_input must return None or raise else _InvalidOutput.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Middleware chains; downloadermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: The downloadermiddlewares __init__.py is empty (1 line) ג€” each module is a standalone, independently-loadable component.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Security boundaries; AGENTS.md > Critical to know; downloader component doc
emitted_claims.text: HTTPS certificate verification is OFF by default: _ScrapyClientContextFactory is non-peer-verifying, DOWNLOAD_VERIFY_CERTIFICATES defaults False, and _ScrapyClientTLSOptions catches VerificationError/CertificateError/ValueError to warn-not-fail.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Security boundaries; downloader component doc
emitted_claims.text: HTTPS-over-proxy uses an HTTP CONNECT tunnel (_TunnelingTCP4ClientEndpoint/_TunnelingAgent); HTTPS proxies for HTTPS destinations are explicitly unsupported.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Security boundaries; AGENTS.md > Critical to know; downloadermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: Cross-origin header-stripping on redirect: Cookie dropped unless same-host + no scheme downgrade; Authorization dropped on any scheme/host/port change; proxy creds stripped on scheme change.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Security boundaries; http2 component doc
emitted_claims.text: HTTP/2 hostname firewall: a stream refuses to send unless the request URL netloc matches the connection base URI host/netloc/ip, else closes INVALID_HOSTNAME; ALPN enforcement accepts only b'h2'.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Security boundaries; spidermiddlewares component doc
emitted_claims.text: Referrer-policy import-path classes from the response header are forbidden (allow_import_path=False), allowed only from trusted settings.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Security boundaries; spiders/downloadermiddlewares component docs
emitted_claims.text: Decompression-bomb guards: httpcompression enforces DOWNLOAD_MAXSIZE raising IgnoreRequest on _DecompressionMaxSizeExceeded; SitemapSpider gunzips under a max_size cap, silently dropping a sitemap on overflow.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Security boundaries; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: Telnet security: when TELNETCONSOLE_PASSWORD is unset a random 8-byte hex password is generated per run and logged at INFO; the console exposes a live Python manhole over engine/crawler/stats.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Data model (scrapy.http); http component doc
emitted_claims.text: bytes is the on-the-wire boundary type: Response.body rejects str; Headers normalizes all keys/values to bytes; decoding to text is exclusively TextResponse's job.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Data model (scrapy.http); http component doc
emitted_claims.text: Text-only shortcuts gated by subtype: base Response.css/xpath/jmespath raise NotSupported; only TextResponse and subclasses implement them.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Data model (scrapy.http); http component doc
emitted_claims.text: URL invariant: _set_url runs w3lib safe_url_string (unless meta['verbatim_url']) and raises 'Missing scheme' unless the URL has :// or is about:/data:.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Data model (scrapy.http); http component doc
emitted_claims.text: NO_CALLBACK sentinel marks requests with no spider callback and raises RuntimeError if ever invoked.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Data model (scrapy.http); http component doc
emitted_claims.text: scrapy.Request and scrapy.http.Request are the SAME class: the root package imports the model FROM scrapy.http, so scrapy.http is the canonical home and scrapy.Request is an alias.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Data model (scrapy.http); http component doc
emitted_claims.text: The leaf data layer imports only scrapy.utils, scrapy.selector (lazy), scrapy.link, scrapy.exceptions and root scrapy for typing ג€” never engine/downloader/middleware/pipelines; it is imported BY them.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Spiders; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: Async-iterator start contract: start() is an async def yielding Request/items via AsyncIterator (versionadded 2.13); default reads start_urls and yields Request(url, dont_filter=True).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Spiders; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: Mandatory name invariant: construction raises ValueError('... must have a name') if neither passed nor class-set.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Spiders; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: Framework callback contract: core calls the private _parse indirection (not parse directly); Spider._parse delegates to parse, subclasses override _parse for rule/feed/sitemap dispatch; the scraper binds to it.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Spiders; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: No-downward-coupling layering: spiders import http/settings/utils/selector/linkextractors but never import scrapy.core.* at runtime (only core coupling is inbound).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Spiders; spiders/http component docs; architecture.md
emitted_claims.text: Circular-import workaround invariant: Spider.logger imports SpiderLoggerAdapter inside the property body with a '# circular import' note.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Spiders; spiders component doc
emitted_claims.text: CrawlSpider rule engine: _compile_rules deep-copies each rule into self._rules so per-instance compilation never mutates the class-level rules tuple.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Pipelines; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: Pipelines are pluggable, priority-ordered components discovered from ITEM_PIPELINES and composed via build_component_list; ordering (not hard-coded calls) defines the chain.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Pipelines; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: Terminal/consumer end of item flow: pipelines receive already-scraped items, transform or drop them, never schedule crawl requests themselves; media fetches are delegated outward to engine.download_async.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Pipelines; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: Media requests are isolated from spider callback flow: created with callback=NO_CALLBACK and have callback/errback stripped before download so pipeline downloads never re-enter parsing.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Pipelines; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: Optional-dependency boundary: a backend/feature self-disables by raising NotConfigured rather than crashing (missing botocore, unset FILES_STORE/IMAGES_STORE, missing Pillow).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Pipelines; pipelines component doc
emitted_claims.text: Media deduplication invariant: concurrent same-fingerprint media requests are coalesced ג€” first triggers the download, later ones attach a Deferred to info.waiting[fp]; result cached in info.downloaded[fp] and all waiters fired once.

emitted_claims.source_region: pipelines component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: Conditional-download freshness: FilesPipeline._onsuccess skips re-download when stored file age (time.time()-last_modified) is within self.expires days.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extensions; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: Extension self-gating: an extension that should not run raises scrapy.exceptions.NotConfigured in __init__/from_crawler rather than loading inert.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extensions; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: The extensions package does NOT load itself: extensions are registered in EXTENSIONS_BASE and loaded by ExtensionManager which lives OUTSIDE this dir at scrapy/extension.py:18; the scrapy/extensions/__init__.py is empty.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extensions; extensions component doc
emitted_claims.text: AutoThrottle algorithm deliberately will NOT decrease delay on non-200 responses to avoid positive-feedback delay collapse.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > CLI; cmdline component doc
emitted_claims.text: Reactor selection is automatic: AsyncCrawlerProcess when TWISTED_REACTOR is the asyncio reactor and FORCE_CRAWLER_PROCESS is off, else CrawlerProcess.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > CLI; cmdline component doc
emitted_claims.text: All commands subclass abstract ScrapyCommand; run() and short_desc() are @abstractmethod.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > CLI; cmdline component doc
emitted_claims.text: Command discovery is convention-based: one Command class per module under scrapy.commands, named by the module's last path segment, excluding base classes.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > CLI; cmdline component doc
emitted_claims.text: Exit-code protocol: each command sets self.exitcode, execute() passes it to sys.exit(); crawl/runspider set 1 on bootstrap_failed; UsageError exits 2.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Utils; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: Dual concurrency model: scrapy.utils branches on is_asyncio_available() to pick asyncio vs Twisted primitives (call_later, create_looping_call, run_in_thread).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Utils; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: deferred_from_coro is the core Deferred<->coroutine bridge, switching by reactor.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Utils; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: build_from_crawler prefers objcls.from_crawler(crawler, ...) else __init__, raising TypeError on a None result (the framework from_crawler convention).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Utils; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: load_object resolves an absolute dotted path to a class/function/variable/instance ג€” the canonical plugin/component-loading primitive.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Utils; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: datatypes.py declares a hard layering rule in its docstring: 'This module must not depend on any module outside the Standard Library.'

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Utils; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: Fail-loud boundary: is_asyncio_reactor_installed() raises RuntimeError instead of silently installing a default reactor (versionchanged 2.13).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Utils; utils component doc
emitted_claims.text: On Windows, install_reactor forces WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy (twisted issue #12527 workaround).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Scheduling support; queues component doc
emitted_claims.text: DownloaderAwarePriorityQueue refuses CONCURRENT_REQUESTS_PER_IP != 0 (raises ValueError at construction).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Scheduling support; queues component doc
emitted_claims.text: Resumed crawls must reuse the same priority-queue class: non-dict slot_startprios rejected with an incompatible-priority-queue error.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Scheduling support; queues component doc
emitted_claims.text: Spider discovery is import-time eager: SpiderLoader.__init__ walks all SPIDER_MODULES immediately, warns (not raises) on import/syntax errors only when SPIDER_LOADER_WARN_ONLY, and warns on duplicate spider names.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Contracts; contracts component doc
emitted_claims.text: Contracts are a CLI/check-only layer: invoked solely via the scrapy check command, which sets env SCRAPY_CHECK=true and monkey-patches spidercls.start to yield contract requests (not driven by the crawl engine).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Contracts; contracts component doc
emitted_claims.text: Async-callback exclusion: a callback returning AsyncGenerator/CoroutineType raises TypeError('Contracts don't support async callbacks'), keeping the checker synchronous.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Contracts; contracts component doc
emitted_claims.text: Contract request construction invariant: built requests force dont_filter=True (so one URL can test multiple callbacks) and set callback=method.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extensibility core; signals component doc
emitted_claims.text: Per-component graceful degradation: NotConfigured while loading a middleware/add-on disables only that one (logged), never aborts the chain.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extensibility core; signals component doc
emitted_claims.text: The middleware chain is async-first: _process_chain is async def, awaiting each method via ensure_awaitable, optionally injecting the spider as the trailing arg.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extensibility core; signals component doc
emitted_claims.text: Signals are duck-typed (a signal is any object); PyDispatcher is the chosen backend, platform-split in the manifest (PyDispatcher on CPython, PyPyDispatcher on PyPy).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Item-layer specifics; item component doc
emitted_claims.text: Field-name allowlist: setting an undeclared key raises KeyError; only names in fields are accepted.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Item-layer specifics; item component doc
emitted_claims.text: ItemMeta metaclass collects all Field-typed class attributes into the fields dict at class creation.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Item-layer specifics; item component doc
emitted_claims.text: Item is a MutableMapping[str, Any] dict-like but NOT a dict.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Item-layer specifics; item component doc
emitted_claims.text: Adapter boundary: exporters access every item only through itemadapter.ItemAdapter/is_item, staying type-agnostic across dict/dataclass/attrs/pydantic/Item.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extraction-layer specifics; selector component doc
emitted_claims.text: Selector type is inferred from response class (xml for XmlResponse, else html); explicit type= forces it.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extraction-layer specifics; selector component doc
emitted_claims.text: Public API alias: LxmlLinkExtractor is exported as LinkExtractor, and lxml.html is the only/default extractor.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extraction-layer specifics; selector component doc
emitted_claims.text: Only schemes {http, https, file, ftp} are followable by the link extractor.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md > Extraction-layer specifics; selector component doc
emitted_claims.text: A large default extension blocklist IGNORED_EXTENSIONS is applied unless overridden by deny_extensions.

emitted_claims.source_region: architecture.md > Component decomposition; settings component doc
emitted_claims.text: Command discovery is convention-based (one Command class per module, named by the module's last path segment).

emitted_claims.source_region: http2 component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: HTTP/2 client stream ids are odd, generated by itertools.count(start=1, step=2) per RFC 7540 ֲ§5.1.1.

emitted_claims.source_region: http2 component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: HTTP/2 idle timeout is 240s via Twisted TimeoutMixin; on timeout the connection is closed with PROTOCOL_ERROR if streams are open else NO_ERROR, then errbacks DownloadTimeoutError.

emitted_claims.source_region: downloader component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: Throttling is per-slot, not global: concurrency uses ip_concurrency or domain_concurrency per slot; needs_backout() is the only global cap (len(active) >= total_concurrency).

emitted_claims.source_region: downloader component doc > Boundary rules / Key facts
emitted_claims.text: Handler scheme protocol: every handler exposes async download_request(request)->Response (+ optional close() and a lazy flag); an unknown/failed scheme raises NotSupported rather than crashing the loop.

emitted_claims.source_region: downloader component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: DOWNLOAD_HANDLERS_BASE wires the scheme->class map (data/file/http/https/s3/ftp); http & https both map to HTTP11DownloadHandler.

emitted_claims.source_region: spidermiddlewares component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: referer.py is a near-complete implementation of the W3C Referrer-Policy spec: 8 named policy classes plus a scrapy-default variant, selected per-request from meta -> response header -> settings.

emitted_claims.source_region: spidermiddlewares component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: HttpError is both a middleware and an exception type: HttpErrorMiddleware.process_spider_input raises HttpError (an IgnoreRequest subclass) for disallowed statuses; process_spider_exception catches it and swallows by returning ().

emitted_claims.source_region: queues component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: BaseDupeFilter is the documented no-op default (filters nothing); its open/close may return a Twisted Deferred.

emitted_claims.source_region: extensions component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: EXTENSIONS_BASE priorities are all 0 in the manifest ג€” load order is not differentiated by priority here (effectively registry insertion order).

emitted_claims.source_region: extensions component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: HTTP cache RFC2616 freshness ports Mozilla/Firefox heuristics, caches 300/301/308 indefinitely, and serves a stale cached response on a 5xx unless must-revalidate is set.

emitted_claims.source_region: settings component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: default_settings.py is a flat module of UPPERCASE module-level globals; Settings.__init__ ingests it via setmodule, copying every key.isupper() global.

emitted_claims.source_region: cmdline component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: runspider loads a self-contained spider by temporarily prepending the file's dir to sys.path, requiring .py/.pyw, and uses DummySpiderLoader since there is no project.

emitted_claims.source_region: signals component doc > Key facts
emitted_claims.text: Package metadata: Scrapy 2.16.0.

beyond_doc.referent: SETTINGS_PRIORITIES ladder {default:0, command:10, addon:15, project:20, spider:30, cmdline:40}; set only takes effect if incoming priority >= stored

beyond_doc.referent: default reactor twisted.internet.asyncioreactor.AsyncioSelectorReactor; engine can switch to asyncio loop at runtime

beyond_doc.referent: de-dup identity = request fingerprint (canonical url+method+body) via RequestFingerprinterProtocol / RFPDupeFilter
beyond_doc.note: Verified scrapy/dupefilters.py:53-57. The doc never mentions duplicate filtering or fingerprinting. NEUTRAL beyond-doc key-invariant.

beyond_doc.referent: HTTPS cert verification OFF by default; _ScrapyClientContextFactory non-peer-verifying, DOWNLOAD_VERIFY_CERTIFICATES False
beyond_doc.note: Verified scrapy/core/downloader/contextfactory.py:42 (non-peer-verifying class). Security boundary-rule entirely outside the doc's scope.

beyond_doc.referent: HTTP/2 hostname firewall: stream refuses unless request netloc matches connection base URI host/netloc/ip; ALPN accepts only b'h2'
beyond_doc.note: Verified scrapy/core/http2/stream.py:199-207 (check_request_url). Doc has no HTTP/2 layer at all. Beyond-doc.

beyond_doc.referent: Scheduler JOBDIR persistence: requests.queue/ dir + active.json startprios state, read on open / written on close
beyond_doc.note: Verified scrapy/core/scheduler.py:480-498 (_dqdir/_read_dqs_state/active.json). The doc describes the Scheduler's role but not its on-disk job-state. Beyond-doc data-store.

beyond_doc.referent: scrapy.Request and scrapy.http.Request are the SAME class; scrapy.http is canonical, scrapy.Request an alias
beyond_doc.note: Verified scrapy/__init__.py:10 (from scrapy.http import ... Request). Implementation-aliasing fact not in the architecture doc.

beyond_doc.referent: single MiddlewareManager ABC subclassed by Downloader/Spider/Extension/ItemPipeline managers
beyond_doc.note: Grounds scrapy/middleware.py:35 + subclasses. Doc lists downloader/spider middlewares separately but never the shared ABC unification. Beyond-doc component-role.

scoring_provenance.method: isolated-agent scoring (separation of duties): a key-reader + an EC-extractor (BLIND to the key) + one matcher per RC + a faithfulness refuter per overlap (vs CODE) + a beyond-doc finder. Workflow  (fact-keyed rescore).
scoring_provenance.orchestrator_caveat: The orchestrating session authored the curator keys, so it is not a fully fresh scorer; but every EC-extraction, match, and faithfulness JUDGMENT was made by an isolated agent (the EC-extractor blind to the key). Recorded honestly.
scoring_provenance.reconciliation: SHARED-EC DEDUP: the matcher pointed RC-002, RC-008, RC-015 all at the broad overview EC-003, whose one incidental inaccuracy (it states the ExecutionEngine constructor instantiates the Scheduler; in code the Downloader (engine.py:137) and Scraper (engine.py:149) are created in __init__ but the Scheduler is created later in open_spider) was charged 3x. Each refuter note confirms the RC's OWN referent fact is code-correct (RC-002 scheduler-exists, RC-008 engine-controls-flow, RC-015 engine<->scheduler-edge all code-backed). Per protocol faithfulness scope, the defect is counted ONCE on RC-015 (the edge it concerns, MEDIUM, not HIGH); RC-002/RC-008 are faithful MATCHes. No HIGH wrong (hard gate holds).

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness
training-contamination — scrapy is a public repo; the blind output may be informed by pretraining, so the number is INDICATIVE.
